text: Agile methodology is a modern approach to managing projects, especially in software development. Work is divided into short phases called sprints, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint starts with planning, where the team selects tasks from a list known as the backlog. These tasks are often described as user stories—simple explanations of features from the customer's perspective. Every day, the team holds brief standup meetings to discuss progress and any problems. After each sprint, the team reviews the work and gets feedback, allowing for improvements in the next sprint. This cycle of iterative development helps teams respond quickly to changing needs.
